_doc: W5B R1 portfolio allocator limits (SHADOW ONLY).
READ THIS BEFORE USING ANY NUMBER HERE. Two of these ceilings -- firm.gross_exposure
and firm.per_sector -- are DERIVED, not ratified. They carry basis:'shadow_derivation_2026-07'
and ratified:false. They exist to make would-reject evidence possible; they are NOT
risk limits anybody signed off on, and nothing in W5B enforces them (there is no
enforce path in the code at all). The enforce-era numbers get their own Pending Action.
CURRENCY (PLAN_W5B §12.2). Every cap here is USD notional. The book is USD-priced
(positions_truth rows are all currency:USD; price_cache is USD) but the only
currency_ok account equity is CAD, and NO FX rate exists (portfolio_snapshot:
usd_ok=false, usdcad_rate_used=null). So caps are deliberately NOT expressed as a
multiple of equity -- gross/equity would be a mixed-unit ratio, wrong by roughly the
USDCAD spread (~0.68x vs ~0.93x). equity_basis below is context, never a divisor.
BINDING vs REPORT-ONLY. A cap with binds:true produces WOULD_REJECT evidence. A cap
with binds:false is computed and reported but never would-rejected -- because
would-rejecting against a number nobody sourced would poison the corpus with

equity_basis.source_note: The same artifact and the same currency_ok gate tier_manager.py:587 reads, fail-closed. PLAN_W5B P13 originally cited pnl_state.json::account_equity as CAD/currency_ok -- corrected in §12 C4: pnl_state.json carries no currency key at all.
equity_basis.usd_note: total_equity_usd_authoritative is null and usdcad_rate_used is null. There is no FX rate in the system, so this CAD figure CANNOT be compared to the USD caps below.
equity_basis.refresh: read live each cycle; never hardcode the balance in code

derivation: OPERATOR_VERIFY 1, ruled 2026-07-23 (PLAN_W5B §13.1).
Anchor: policy.py:665 max_total_exposure = $500,000 USD -- the only
USD-denominated firm-level exposure number in the repo. It is ENFORCED today,
but at PER-ORDER grain, which is exactly why it cannot express a portfolio
ceiling: the live book gross is $671,037, already 1.34x it.
Tier headroom: live tier is SCALE (runtime/tier_state.json; band CAD 226,560
-> 14,160,000, demotion floor CAD 203,904). SCALE's risk_profile is ALL-NULL,
so the tier imposes no competing notional ceiling and the derivation must
supply its own discipline.
Chosen: 1.5 x the enforced per-order gross = $750,000. It sits ABOVE the live
book ($78,963 of headroom) so the corpus is not saturated on day one and every
row is a genuine MARGINAL verdict; and BELOW the only existing aggregate
reference (margin_block 2.0x NetLiq). A ceiling below the live book would
would-reject every intent forever and carry no information.

reference_only.margin_block_source: config/margin_block.json:11 aggregate gross (margin/NetLiq basis, shadow, a DIFFERENT lens from delta-dollars)

net_exposure.note: D3: NO net-exposure notional cap exists anywhere in the repo. Net is computed and reported every cycle but never would-rejected. Proposal for a future PA: 1.0x equity -- which requires an FX rate that does not exist yet.
net_exposure.live_observation: Net == gross == $671,037 today: every one of the 11 positions is long. PSQ, the only genuinely short-beta ticket, is $265 (0.04% of gross), and SVXY is a LONG short-vol position, i.e. risk-ON rather than a hedge.

per_symbol_concentration.pct_note: The 5% leg is NOT applied: it would require dividing a USD exposure by a CAD equity. Recorded for the enforce-era PA, when an FX rate exists.
per_symbol_concentration.live_observation: LLY ($215,488) and SPY ($182,637) ALREADY exceed this enforced cap, because it is per-ORDER and a position accumulated over several orders never meets it. This is the missing-aggregate-layer thesis on live data -- and it means day-one would-rejects come from a hard-sourced, already-ratified number.

derivation: OPERATOR_VERIFY 2, ruled 2026-07-23 (PLAN_W5B §13.2).
Rule: no single sector may exceed half the firm gross ceiling.
Against the live book that puts healthcare ($316,936) at 84.5% of its cap with
$58,064 of headroom -- inside the limit today, so the NEXT healthcare ticket is
what trips it. That is the marginal concentration signal R1 exists to produce.

superseded_source.why_not_used: Small-account calibration. Against a $671k book all six live sectors breach it, which is saturation, not evidence. Retained here so the number is visibly superseded rather than quietly dropped.

per_sector.unmapped_note: The 'unmapped' sector is count-only and NEVER binds -- the W4A LC3 idiom (PLAN_W4A §4). A symbol missing from config/symbol_sectors.json must not create a blockable bucket nobody ratified.

KRAKEN.note: The dynamic cap EXCEEDS the wallet, so the wallet is the true binding constraint. Cited from the artifacts; re-read live at evaluation.

IBKR.note: No IBKR-specific venue cap exists anywhere. IBKR exposure falls under firm.gross_exposure.

note: Ruled 2026-07-23 (PLAN_W5B §13.3): static sector buckets are ratified; rolling
correlation is deferred to R2 with real sample depth. NO rho is computed anywhere
in W5B and no numeric correlation appears in any artifact, so there is no fake
precision to mistake for a measurement.
The reason for deferral is NOT that correlation data is sparse (it is not -- all 11
book symbols carry ~251 daily bars) and NOT that no threshold is sourced (one IS:
see existing_per_order_reducer). It is that a second, independently-thresholded
correlation layer at portfolio grain would DOUBLE-COUNT the same effect through two
unreconciled numbers. R2's first task is to reconcile with the existing reducer,

existing_per_order_reducer.measured_book_stat: config/sizing_config.json notes: 'a book averaging 0.654 pairwise correlation'

scr_sizing_reference._doc: Reference only. The allocator OBSERVES; it never re-applies SCR sizing.

coach.note: operator_verify. Alert only on the Nth CONSECUTIVE would-reject on the SAME limit dimension -- never per intent (that is the R13 flood CTF-T2 fixed).
